findings.evidence: `Basic.lean` has the general API first at lines 107-125 (`add_star_self_nonneg` then `map_zero_nonneg`); this file proves only the special case at lines 73-85, while `isPositiveDefiniteKernel_apply_self_nonneg hF p` directly gives the general diagonal.
findings.fix: Add the general `add_star_self_nonneg`/`add_star_self_im`/`add_star_self_re_nonneg` analogue first and derive the existing zero lemmas by specializing `p = (0,0)`.
findings.issue: `map_zero_nonneg`, `map_zero_im`, and `map_zero_re_nonneg` expose only the special case `(0,0)`, but the kernel diagonal gives the natural BCR norm-point statement for every `p : ℝ≥0 × V`, namely nonnegativity/reality of `F (p.1 + p.1, p.2 - p.2)` (equivalently `F (p.1 + p.1, 0)`).

summary: The new predicate is mostly at the intended BCR specialization level, but the origin API is under-generalized relative to the existing positive-definite API.
